layman_headline: Extreme fear grips the market, but none of today's candidates are worth the risk.
layman_explanation: The crypto market is in 'Extreme Fear' mode with a score of just 22 out of 100. While fear often creates buying opportunities, the coins available today are either ultra-low-liquidity tokenized stocks, brand-new tokens with no chart history, or coins that have already pumped massively. U.S.-Iran tensions and Bank of Japan rate hike warnings are adding macro uncertainty. Bitcoin itself is showing some recovery (+4.5% over 7 days), but the altcoin candidates are simply too risky with no reliable technical data to define proper entries and stop-losses.
layman_action: Stay in cash and wait for a higher-quality setup. The fear in the market is real and driven by geopolitical events — patience here protects your capital until a clear opportunity emerges.
expert_reasoning: The candidate pool is exceptionally poor this cycle. ARROW is up 250% in 24h with zero 7-day data and no chart analysis — classic pump with no definable stop-loss. SNDKB and MUB are tokenized stocks with negligible crypto-native volume. TRIA has the most data but is down 73.7% from ATH, below all moving averages, and the Morning Star pattern on the chart conflicts with bearish price structure (price below all MAs). NES already ran 31.6% in 7 days and is likely extended. ZEST and UMXM lack any technical indicators. The Fear & Greed index at 22 is a historically reliable contrarian signal (75% win rate in our data), but it needs to be paired with a quality setup — which doesn't exist here. Geopolitical risk from U.S.-Iran tensions and BOJ rate hike warnings add macro headwinds.
risk_factors: U.S.-Iran geopolitical escalation could trigger broader market sell-off and push fear even lower
Bank of Japan rate hike acceleration could strengthen yen carry trade unwind, pressuring all risk assets
All swing candidates have zero or negligible trading volume, creating extreme slippage and exit risk
watch_list: Fear & Greed index movement — if it drops below 15, a major capitulation bounce setup may form in BTC or ETH
U.S.-Iran conflict resolution or escalation — this is the primary macro driver right now
BTC price action around $60,000 support — a hold there with volume would signal a safer entry for large-caps
market_context: The market is in Extreme Fear (22/100) driven by U.S.-Iran tensions and BOJ rate hike warnings. BTC is recovering modestly (+4.5% weekly) but altcoins are under heavy pressure with BTC dominance extremely elevated.
context_banner: Extreme Fear creates opportunity, but today's candidate pool lacks the liquidity and technical structure needed for a responsible swing trade entry.

key_signals.description: Extreme Fear at 22/100 with improving trend (up from 20 yesterday) — historically a reliable contrarian buy signal (75% win rate), but requires a quality coin to pair with.
U.S.-Iran conflict entering second day, keeping markets volatile; Bitcoin and ether steady but risk-off sentiment dominates.
ETH derivatives reset noted, suggesting positioning is being unwound — could precede a cleaner setup in coming days.
Bank of Japan may accelerate rate hikes above 2%, creating headwinds for all risk assets through yen carry trade unwind pressure.
No swing candidate shows meaningful volume data — all have null volume_vs_avg, making any entry a blind trade with no confirmation.
